title: Spot Price Optimizer
description: > ℹ️ Note: Loxone provides direct integration with leading European spot markets, including EPEX, OTE, and ESIOS. This enables a reliable, efficient, and seamless setup without the need for additional configuration.

inputs.description: Pulse: Outputs are reset / switched off. On: Block is locked. Dominating input.
Starts the automatic. The output O will be activated as often as defined by 'demand' within 'period'.

inputs.summary: Relative Mode: Price in the hour now +0 to +23
inputs.description: Price forecast for the hour now + offset. This inputs are not available when using the Spot market mode.

outputs.description: Output will be activated at the hours with the lowest price after a trigger.
The current price is Very High compared to the other hours or above the Max parameter. Limits will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The current price is High compared to the other hours. Limits will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The current price is Low compared to the other hours. Limits will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The current price is Very Low compared to the other hours. Limits will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The maximum price will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The minimum price will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The average price will be recalculated after a Trigger based on the forecast values available at that moment.
The price of the next period.
Intelligent API based connector. API Commands

parameters.description: If the current price is above this value, the very High output will always activate.
Value which can be used in the formula with I2.
Value which can be used in the formula with I3.

properties.description: Use a formula to calculate the actual price you are paying. Ask your energy supplier for the formula. I1 = price from the inputs or the spot market price (without tax). I2 = I2 parameter. I3 = I3 parameter. I4 = Minutes since midnight for which the price is calculated.

Relative: Inputs provide the price relative to the current hour. Absolute: Inputs provide the price for each hour of the day (00:00 to 23:00). Spot market: Data is obtained from european energy spot markets, Inputs for hourly prices are hidden.
Spot market area for which to fetch the prices.
Interval at which Spot Market prices will be retrieved.
